title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 slip as Trump stays firm on August tariff deadline, announces copper duties Investors are digesting Trump's stiff tariffs on imports from more than a dozen countries and delayed the return of sweeping April levies.

Analysis-Nine months in, Starbucks CEO faces tall order in turnaround (Reuters) -Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol earned a reputation on Wall Street as a miracle worker for wounded restaurant brands like Taco Bell and Chipotle.  Nine months into his Starbucks tenure, investors are unsure if lightning will strike a third time.  Shares jumped more than 21% on August 13, the day Niccol was named CEO, on hopes he would inject the company with new vitality after several quarters of falling sales and pressure from activist investor Elliott Investment Management.
Trending tickers: Nvidia, Intel, Apple, Antofagasta, and WPP The latest investor updates on stocks that are trending on Wednesday.
Gold slips to lowest in over a week as Trump tariff threats push dollar higher Trump's tariffs stir volatility as pound drops and oil climbs.
